mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS trends downward, discount rates and financing costs fall, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not precede positive 20-day SPX performance.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of observations when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly readings, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in CPIAUCSL raises inflation pressure, forcing tighter policy expectations and lowering equity multiples, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its prior 6-month average by at least 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average forward return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and deteriorating demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the mean forward return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support equity earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in periods of rising RSAFS growth, or if the signal loses positive association in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST is also rising, it implies synchronized industrial and housing expansion, reinforcing cyclical growth expectations and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint condition is required because either series alone is weaker.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST trigger does not outperform single-series triggers, or if triggered periods fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns above baseline frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after prior expansion marks an activity slowdown that eventually compresses earnings expectations and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdowns are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips positive in repeated rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, falling PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE captures payroll contraction and slack accumulation, which should depress investor risk appetite and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-loss/unemployment-rise condition does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns better than either series alone, or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from chance.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL indicates policy pressure against inflation, which tightens financial conditions and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above its 3-month average while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 2% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation episodes fail to produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional short-horizon mean.
